signature_questions.what_they_press_on: Requests re-opening timing, demand quality (volume and rate), mix of onboard/pre-board spend and relative EBITDA/APCD contribution as China returns.

topics.what_they_press_on: Requests re-opening timing, demand quality (volume and rate), mix of onboard/pre-board spend and relative EBITDA/APCD contribution as China returns.
Seeks cadence and EBITDA contribution timing for TUI (how it ramps back to 2019 contribution levels).

distinctive_concerns.blind_spots: No analyst questions in the history explicitly address fuel‑hedging / bunker fuel hedges (0 turns on fuel hedging despite peers often asking commodity/hedge questions in Q3/Q4 seasons) and no focused questions on loyalty-program KPIs (repeat-booking rates, member cohorts) despite repeated discussion of loyalty in management comments. n_turns < 2 for these topics; insufficient to characterize deeper stance.
distinctive_concerns.stance_drift: Early 2022 (2022-Q1/2022-Q2) emphasis was on demand recovery and booking-window dynamics and immediate protocol impacts; by 2023 (2023-Q1/2023-Q3) he shifts to capacity allocation, pricing decomposition, and China re-opening economics (compare booking/mix focus in 2022-Q1 to China/APCD and capacity allocation focus in 2023-Q1 and 2023-Q3).

coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Titles progress from 'Equity Analyst' to 'Director' and 'Director and Senior Equity Research Analyst' across 2022–2023 (e.g., 2022-Q1 listed as 'Equity Analyst, Wells Fargo'; 2022-Q3 onward shows 'Director' or 'Director and Senior'). Management addresses him by first name 'Dan' and repeatedly yields time for multiple turns, suggesting a mid-to-senior sell-side role.
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Primary coverage is cruises/leisure travel with repeated questions about bookings, capacity deployment, pricing, and China re-opening; by 2023 his remit is described as 'Gaming, Lodging, and Leisure', showing a broader leisure/travel lens.
rhetorical_signature: Opener: frequently begins informally — opens with 'Hey, good morning' in 6 of 17 calls (e.g., 2022-Q1, 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2022-Q4, 2023-Q1, 2023-Q3).
Frequent verbal tic/acknowledgement: uses 'Got it.' as a transition or closer in multiple turns (examples: 'Got it.' in 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2023-Q3).
Tone: concise, collegial, and follow-up oriented — typically frames multi-part, practical questions (demand -> mix -> implication).

reasoning_style.justification: He blends quantitative asks (requests for % bookings, 'mid-single digits', '1%-2%' cost impacts, APCD/EBITDA asks) with strategic deployment questions (capacity allocation, product mix, China relaunch). Examples: 2022-Q2 asks about 'mid-single digits' net cruise costs and 2023-Q1 asks about capacity allocation between Caribbean and Europe.
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi-part questions are common; ≈ 9 of 17 turns contain ≥2 question parts (examples: 2022-Q1, 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2023-Q1, 2023-Q3). He regularly takes follow-up turns for clarification or deeper drill rather than confrontational pushback. Push-back is rare; most turns are exploratory or seeking granularity.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He asks for specific percentages and guidance repeatedly — explicit % requests appear in at least 6 analyst turns (examples: asking 'what's the typical % of bookings' in 2022-Q3; '1%-2% cost increase' in 2022-Q4; references to 'mid-single digits' in 2022-Q2). He also requests mechanisms and like‑for‑like decompositions (e.g., 2023-Q1 'pre-board spend versus onboard' and 'EBITDA relative ... per APCD yields').
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He frequently anchors to management's recent phrases/guidance and to pre‑COVID/2019 baselines. Examples: he references management's 'mid-single digits' cost comment (2022-Q2) and asks about the historical '% booked' benchmark that management quoted as '55% and 60%' (2022-Q3). There is no strong evidence of anchoring to peer names (NCLH/CCL) in the history provided.

queue_behavior.typical_position: #4–#9 of the queue across calls; distribution by position (of 17 turns): #4:1, #5:3, #6:4, #7:2, #8:4, #9:3 — most common positions are #6–#8 (11 of 17 turns).
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When early to speak (e.g., 2022-Q1 when he was #4) he opens on demand mix and booking cadence — example question: 'How would you break that out between ... new cruise customers ... longer term bookings or short-end bookings?' (2022-Q1).

adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reframe_deeper: Evidence: when peers/management already discussed China/bookings he asks for a decomposition or economics — e.g., 'pre-board spend versus onboard, the EBITDA relative to the rest of your portfolio per APCD yields' (2023-Q1).
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reactive_followup: Evidence: he explicitly references management wording to drill deeper — e.g., picks up on management's 'mid-single digits' phrase to ask pacing and 2023 outlook ('I think you said mid-single digits') (2022-Q2).
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.stay_on_topic_anyway: Evidence: even after earlier Q&A coverage of bookings/mix in a call, he still asks the bookings 'what's the typical % of bookings that you have on the books' (2022-Q3) instead of pivoting away.

explicit_peer_references.note: No evidence in the provided history of explicit references to other analysts' questions (phrases like 'following up on X's question' do not appear in his verbatim turns).

management_line_anchoring.note: He explicitly cites management's phrasing in his question at least once (2022-Q2 'mid-single digits'). Other turns implicitly build on management guidance but do not quote a specific phrase.

operator_or_followup_signal.behavior: He routinely takes a second (or additional) turn within the same call to clarify or drill deeper rather than to change topic.
examples: 2022-Q2 (asked net cruise costs then immediately asked about marketing ramp)
2022-Q4 (asked initial cost/one-time items and then followed with a TUI follow-up)
2023-Q1 (asked Caribbean/short-product allocation then followed with China/APCD drill)

framing_overrides_by_theme.cost_cadence_dry_dock: Asks for pacing of NCC in H2 and into next year with inflation overlay.

_built_from: v7: company-layer split + probe/topic structure + v6 prose
